page: Amazon Web Services x8i.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache resources include a 480 MB L3 cache. Network connectivity is supported by a 75 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with the server placing in the top 10% for multi-core stress-ng, PassMark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, achieving top-tier status in read-only operations for both small and large blocks. The processor's support for Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 contributes to top-tier prompt processing speeds in LLM inference across small, medium, and large models. However, single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores fall into the average category, highlighting a performance profile optimized for parallel execution rather than single-threaded speed.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x8i.metal-48xl offers high resource density for memory-intensive enterprise applications, though the lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage. This server is designed for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and LLM prompt processing. It is less suitable for workloads requiring local NVMe storage or high single-threaded performance.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 192 vCPUs, 96 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores, and 3072.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating at 3.9 GHz, the processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. The server provides a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory read bandwidth, and rapid LLM prompt processing, though single-core performance remains average. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for massive parallel processing and memory-intensive workloads, making it ideal for in-memory databases, large-scale software compilation, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 3072 GB DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform featuring massive DDR5 memory and multi-core Intel Xeon processors for parallelized, memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 96 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz
3072.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory providing 16.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory read bandwidth, and database performance
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
Optimized for in-memory databases, large-scale compilation, and LLM prompt processing
